title: Improve Issue Parent-Child Linking
description: AI agents using sl issue create fail to set --parent during task creation, resulting in orphaned issues. Add parent link type, orphan detection, bulk reparent, and updated skill instructions.

title: US1: Link Parent via Issue Link Command
description: WHY: sl issue link only supports blocks/related but not parent. Users and AI agents must use sl issue update --parent instead, which is inconsistent.
design: Add parent case to link command switch in pkg/cli/commands/issue.go. Route through store.Update() with ParentID set, reusing existing validation (existence, self-parent, cycle detection).
acceptance_criteria: sl issue link <child> parent <parent> sets parent-child relationship. Existing validation (cycles, self-parent, existence) applies.

title: Add parent case to link command switch
description: WHY: sl issue link does not support parent as a link type, forcing users to use sl issue update --parent for parent-child relationships.

items.item: parent case added to link command switch

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/issue.go (link command handler, around line 1589-1632). Add a case for parent in the linkType switch. Instead of calling store.AddDependency(), call store.Update(fromID, IssueUpdate{ParentID: &toID}) to reuse all existing parent validation (existence check, self-parent rejection, cycle detection via wouldCreateParentCycle).
acceptance_criteria: sl issue link <child-id> parent <parent-id> sets the child ParentID. Errors on nonexistent parent, self-parent, or circular chain. Visible in sl issue show.

title: US4: Update AI Skill Instructions for --parent
description: WHY: AI agents generating issues via /specledger.tasks do not consistently use --parent, resulting in orphaned issues (22/39 in observed case).
design: File: .claude/commands/specledger.tasks.md. Add bold/emphasized instructions that --parent is MANDATORY for all non-epic issues. Add validation reminder after issue creation to check for orphans.
acceptance_criteria: Skill instructions explicitly state --parent is required for all feature and task issues. Examples show --parent usage.

title: Emphasize --parent as mandatory in specledger.tasks skill
description: WHY: AI agents skip --parent during issue creation because skill instructions do not emphasize it strongly enough, causing orphaned issues.
design: File: .claude/commands/specledger.tasks.md. Add CRITICAL/bold section near CLI examples stating --parent is MANDATORY for all non-epic issues. Add a post-creation validation step reminding agents to verify no orphaned issues exist.
acceptance_criteria: Skill file contains explicit mandatory --parent instruction. All CLI examples for feature and task creation include --parent flag.

title: US2: Warn About Orphaned Issues
description: WHY: After bulk issue creation, users have no way to identify non-epic issues missing parent relationships without manual inspection.
design: Add Orphaned bool to ListFilter in pkg/issues/issue.go. Add filtering logic in pkg/issues/store.go List(). Add --orphaned flag to list command in pkg/cli/commands/issue.go.
acceptance_criteria: sl issue list --orphaned shows only non-epic issues with no parent. Epics excluded. Empty result shows success message.

title: Add Orphaned field to ListFilter struct
description: WHY: The ListFilter struct needs an Orphaned field to support filtering for non-epic issues without parents.
design: File: pkg/issues/issue.go (ListFilter struct, around line 97-106). Add Orphaned bool field alongside existing Blocked bool. An orphaned issue is defined as: IssueType != epic AND ParentID == nil.
acceptance_criteria: ListFilter has Orphaned bool field. Field is accessible from list command.

title: Add orphan filtering logic to store List method
description: WHY: The store List() method needs to filter issues based on the new Orphaned flag in ListFilter.

items.item: Orphan filter logic added to List method
Filter composes correctly with other filters

design: File: pkg/issues/store.go (List method). When filter.Orphaned is true, include only issues where IssueType is not epic AND ParentID is nil. Apply this filter alongside existing status/type/priority filters.
acceptance_criteria: store.List() with Orphaned=true returns only non-epic issues with nil ParentID.

title: Add --orphaned flag to issue list command
description: WHY: Users need a CLI flag to filter for orphaned issues without writing custom queries.

items.item: Success message when no orphans found

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/issue.go (list command, around line 246-256 for flags, 372-464 for handler). Add --orphaned bool flag. Set filter.Orphaned = true when flag is set. Follow same pattern as existing --blocked flag.
acceptance_criteria: sl issue list --orphaned shows orphaned issues. No orphans shows success message. Works with --all flag for cross-spec detection.

title: US3: Bulk Reparent Issues
description: WHY: Fixing orphaned issues requires N separate sl issue update --parent commands. A bulk command reduces this to a single invocation.
design: Add reparent subcommand to pkg/cli/commands/issue.go. Signature: sl issue reparent <parent-id> <child-id>... Validate parent exists once, loop through children calling store.Update with ParentID, continue on error.
acceptance_criteria: sl issue reparent <parent> <child1> <child2>... sets parent for all children. Errors reported per-child without halting. Summary shows successes and failures.

title: Add reparent subcommand to issue command
description: WHY: Users need to reparent multiple orphaned issues to the same parent in a single command instead of N separate update commands.

items.item: Summary of successes and failures printed

design: File: pkg/cli/commands/issue.go. Add reparent subcommand to issueCmd. Args: <parent-id> <child-id> [child-id...]. Validate parent exists first. Loop through child IDs, call store.Update(childID, IssueUpdate{ParentID: &parentID}) for each. Collect errors and successes. Print summary at end. Continue processing on individual failures (FR-006).
acceptance_criteria: sl issue reparent <parent> <child1> <child2>... sets parent for all valid children. Invalid IDs reported as errors without stopping. Summary shows count of successes and failures.
